title: Restructure the rambling ESG answer answer-first
guidance: Reward an answer-first structure with clean, well-nested bullets. Penalise keeping the build-up or burying the recommendation.

runner_input.instruction: Restructure the note below so the recommendation leads, then the supporting arguments with their evidence.
runner_input.constraints: Output: markdown — a bold recommendation on the first line, then nested bullets (top-level claims, with evidence indented beneath each).

source_blocks.text: We've been doing ESG for a while. There's a screen at the start, and we exclude some sectors like coal and tobacco and controversial weapons. We also put a section in every IC memo. The portfolio companies report some KPIs, six of them, annually, and the board looks at them. We don't have third-party assurance on those yet. So overall we integrate ESG across the process.

rubric_notes: Lead: ESG is integrated across screening, IC and monitoring.
Nest: the screen/exclusions; the IC section; the KPIs (no assurance yet).
